name: Daily Seinfeld Sequence (Entertain-Then-Sell Engagement)
source_books: DotCom Secrets (Russell Brunson) — Seinfeld Sequence + Attractive Character
Copywriting Secrets (Jim Edwards) — hooks, subject lines, Hook/Story/Offer
Marketing Secrets Blackbook — 'publish daily', Stack Don't Switch
role_in_3_closes: The maintenance layer AFTER the Soap Opera Sequence. It keeps the list warm and runs Emotion / Logic / Fear closes on rotation, episode by episode, ascending the value ladder (DotCom Secrets follow-up funnel).

events: Any engaged subscriber on the main list
Buyers who have not yet ascended the value ladder
trigger.entry_conditions: tag 'sos-complete' present OR on main engaged list.
trigger.exit_conditions: Hard bounce / unsubscribe / sustained disengagement (RFMS recency drop) → move to re-engagement or sunset. Never truly 'ends' while subscriber stays engaged.

channels: Optional: social post mirror (publish-daily principle, Blackbook)
cadence: Daily or near-daily, ongoing (DotCom Secrets: the Seinfeld sequence runs indefinitely). Sent as broadcasts or as an evergreen drip for new graduates, then live broadcasts thereafter.

email_anatomy.structure: Every Seinfeld email = Hook → Story → Offer (Edwards 'Hook/Story/Offer'). The email is ~90% entertaining 'episode about nothing' and ends with a smooth segue: 'and that reminds me… here's how I can help → [offer].'
parts: HOOK = curiosity subject line + first line that earns the open (Edwards: 8 of 10 only read the subject).
STORY = a short, entertaining, personal or topical episode (the 'about nothing' part) carrying a single lesson.
SEGUE = one transition sentence that pivots the lesson to the offer.
OFFER = one clear CTA to a product/funnel on the value ladder.

supporting: Jim Edwards — Hook/Story/Offer, curiosity hooks ('unlikely character + timing + result'), one clear CTA
Brunson — Stack Don't Switch (every offer stacks onto the one new opportunity)
attractive_character_elements: Polarity ('love me / hate me — there's no money in the middle')

sequence.beat: Tell a story of a setback and the comeback. Lesson = the principle behind your offer.
sequence.subject_angle: 'The week I lost everything (and what saved me)'
sequence.segue_to: the offer as the 'what saved me'

sequence.beat: Draw a line: the old broken way vs. the new opportunity. Repel the wrong people, attract your tribe.
sequence.persona_formula: Polarity; Edwards good-vs-bad negative (attack the METHOD, never a named person)

sequence.beat: Share a discovery / experiment / result. Curiosity-led.
sequence.subject_angle: 'I tested this for 30 days. The result shocked me.'
sequence.segue_to: the offer is how they get the same discovery faster

sequence.beat: Reveal a behind-the-scenes secret or counter-intuitive truth. LOGIC close flavor — facts/proof.
sequence.subject_angle: 'The thing the gurus won't tell you about [topic]'
sequence.segue_to: offer delivers the secret in full

sequence.beat: Let a customer's story do the selling. Social proof as story.
sequence.subject_angle: 'How Sarah went from [before] to [after] in 6 weeks'

sequence.beat: Start a story today, pay it off tomorrow; offer rides the resolution. FEAR/urgency close flavor when an offer window is closing.
sequence.subject_angle: Day 1: 'I almost didn't hit send on this…' / Day 2: 'Okay, here's what happened'
sequence.segue_to: time-bound offer resolves with the arc

ghl_build.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Contact Tag Added' = 'sos-complete'. Plus: send live broadcasts to the engaged segment from the GHL Campaigns/Email Broadcast tool.
actions: Two-mode build: (A) EVERGREEN — a workflow that drips a rotating bank of Hook/Story/Offer emails to new graduates; (B) LIVE — daily broadcasts to the 'engaged' smart list.
Each email: single CTA to a value-ladder offer; rotate the 7 episode types.
Tag click behavior (e.g. 'interested-[offer]') to feed targeted close sequences.
wait_steps: Evergreen: Wait 1 day between episodes (send window 8:00am contact tz). Live: scheduled broadcast times.
if_else: Engagement smart-list filter: include only contacts who opened ≥1 email in last 30 days (RFMS recency) so deliverability stays high.
IF a contact clicks an offer link 2+ times without buying → enroll in that offer's 'three-closes' or 'scarcity-deadline-close' workflow.
IF no open in 30–45 days → move to re-engagement workflow, then sunset to protect sender reputation.
IF purchase of offer X → suppress offer-X emails, ascend to next value-ladder offer (Stack Don't Switch).
ghl_build.notes: GoHighLevel: maintain an 'Engaged 30-day' smart list for live broadcasts; use the Workflow for the evergreen rotation feeding new graduates. Keep ONE new opportunity and STACK offers onto it (Blackbook Secret 43) — don't introduce a brand-new opportunity each email. Publish daily (Blackbook). Each email obeys Hook/Story/Offer with exactly ONE CTA (Edwards).

integrity_guardrails: Stories must be true; testimonials must be real and provable (Edwards proof rule).
Go negative on the METHOD/old-way, never on a named competitor (Edwards Secret #24).
Protect deliverability: only mail the engaged; sunset dead weight.
kpis: Revenue per email / per subscriber per month
